extraction.one_line_summary: LAFAY Victor attacked at 1km to go on flat finish, holding off VAN AERT Wout's chase to win as POGAČAR took third for time bonus
tactical_patterns: SKJELMOSE attacked with 2km remaining forcing UAE to chase, softening VAN AERT for LAFAY's decisive 1km move
VAN AERT personally closed multiple late attacks instead of preserving sprint position, compromising his own leadout
POGAČAR sprinted for third specifically targeting time bonuses, revealing GC intent on transitional stages
Intermediate sprint showed GROENEWEGEN boxed despite perfect position, suggesting sprint train coordination issues
KELDERMAN led peloton under 1km indicating Jumbo willing to sacrifice GC rider for VAN AERT sprint support

rider_signals.LAFAY Victor: positive: second consecutive day showing exceptional form, first Grand Tour stage win suggests breakthrough condition
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: forced to close gaps himself in final 2km instead of positioning for sprint indicates team support gaps
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: actively sprinting for bonus seconds on flat stages reveals proactive GC aggression beyond mountain stages
rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: positive: attacking with 2km left on flat finish shows tactical maturity beyond typical young rider conservatism
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: boxed in intermediate sprint despite perfect positioning suggests declining bike handling or team coordination
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: contested intermediate sprint heavily but absent from stage finale suggests selective energy management strategy
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: leading peloton under 1km for VAN AERT indicates Jumbo prioritizing stages over protecting GC depth
